name: Impact Story Builder Interview Schema
notes: This schema covers the MVP interview flow only and excludes the future story library.
Validation lengths are pragmatic MVP defaults added to make the schema form-ready.
The source document requires a related resource link in the final story but does not define a matching interview question. That field is deferred from the MVP input schema.

The source document distinguishes exact quotes from draft or paraphrased quotes in generation guidance but does not define a dedicated input control. The MVP generator should treat provided quotes as draft-safe unless a later UI adds quote status.

steps.title: Project details
steps.purpose: Capture the basic project context and what SEI did in one place, while still allowing pasted source text to prefill details when useful.

fields.label: What is the project name and location?
fields.hint: Include the country or region. This anchors the story geographically.
fields.example: Volta Basin Water Security - Ghana, West Africa

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Not provided' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

fields.label: Paste any existing project information here (optional)
fields.hint: Project page, workplan excerpt, grant description, or even a few sentences you have written before. The more you include, the fewer follow-up questions you will need to answer.
fields.example: We worked with the World Bank in Ghana to build a WEAP model for the Volta Basin. The project ran 2022-2024 and included three training workshops.

validationRule.summary: Optional; if present, trim whitespace and keep between 30 and 10000 characters.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'No source text pasted' in muted text on the review screen; do not block progress or generation.
missingStateDisplayRule.generationFallback: Generate from direct interview answers only.

fields.label: What did SEI do in this project?
fields.hint: Think: modeling, training workshops, policy engagement, tool development, research publications, stakeholder facilitation, or partnerships established.
fields.example: Built a WEAP model, ran 3 capacity-building workshops with 45 government staff, and co-authored a policy brief with the Ministry of Water.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Not provided' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

fields.label: Was anything added or changed during the project that was not in the original plan?
fields.hint: Capture adaptive changes, unexpected partnerships, scope expansions, or participant-driven additions.
fields.example: We added a stakeholder scenario exercise after the first workshop because participants asked for it. It became the highlight of the engagement.

validationRule.summary: Optional; if present, trim whitespace and keep between 10 and 1200 characters.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Not provided' in muted text on the review screen; do not block progress or generation.

uiRules: Run analysis before advancing if source text is present.
Mark inferred values so the user can confirm or correct them.
Store a confidence flag per inferred value.

steps.title: Outcomes and impact
steps.purpose: Capture the most important outcome first, then add specificity about who benefited, at what scale, and what enabled success.

fields.label: What was the single most important outcome of this project?
fields.hint: Choose one outcome type first, then describe it in your own words.

validationRule.summary: Required; exactly one option must be selected.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'No outcome type selected' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

fields.label: Describe the most important outcome in your own words
fields.hint: State what changed and make the change concrete.
fields.example: Government agencies now use the WEAP model as the basis for water allocation decisions, which did not happen before the project.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Not provided' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

fields.label: Who benefited, and at what scale?
fields.hint: Think populations, geographic areas, institutions, or species. Include numbers if available, even estimates.
fields.example: 1.2 million people in the Volta Basin across three countries; 12 government agencies; 3 river basin authorities.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Not provided' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

fields.label: What were the enabling conditions that made this success possible?
fields.hint: Think political windows, key champions, institutional trust, timing, the project approach, or partnerships.
fields.example: A new National Water Policy was under development and our findings fed directly into the drafting process, so the timing was critical.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Not provided' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

steps.title: Evidence and voice
steps.purpose: Collect the evidence that separates a specific impact story from a generic one: a quote, two statistics, and a forward-looking statement.

fields.label: Do you have a quote from a partner, beneficiary, or policymaker?
fields.hint: Even a paraphrase is fine. Include who said it and in what context.
fields.example: "The WEAP model gave us a common language to negotiate water allocation across borders." - Ministry Director, Ghana, 2024

validationRule.summary: Optional; if present, trim whitespace and keep between 15 and 600 characters.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Quote not provided' in muted warning text on the review screen; do not block progress, but generation should add [QUOTE NEEDED].

fields.label: Share statistic 1 that shows why this work matters
fields.hint: Use scale of the problem, reach of the solution, before-and-after comparison, number of people or hectares affected, or WEAP user reach.
fields.example: The basin supplies drinking water to 1.2 million people across three countries.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Statistic 1 missing' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

fields.label: Share statistic 2 that shows why this work matters
fields.hint: Use a second data point that reinforces scale, urgency, reach, or before-and-after change.
fields.example: 58,000 WEAP users in 160+ countries rely on tools like the ones we built in this project.

validationRule.summary: Optional but strongly encouraged; if present, trim whitespace and keep between 5 and 220 characters.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Second statistic not provided' in muted warning text on the review screen; do not block progress, but generation should note that a second statistic would strengthen the story.

fields.label: How could this work be applied or scaled in the future?
fields.hint: Think replication in other basins, influence on national policy, embedding in partner institutions, or becoming a regional standard.
fields.example: The model is now managed by the river basin authority and could be expanded to the Upper Volta sub-basins. We have already received inquiries from two neighboring countries.

missingStateDisplayRule.summary: Show 'Not provided' in muted text on the review screen and block step submit and generation until completed.

steps.title: Review and generate
steps.purpose: Show a clean summary of all collected answers, expose gaps before generation, and then create an editable story draft.

outcomeTypeOptions.label: Improved decisions
outcomeTypeOptions.description: Changed how a decision-maker or institution makes choices.

outcomeTypeOptions.label: Changed agendas
outcomeTypeOptions.description: Brought an issue onto the policy or institutional agenda.

outcomeTypeOptions.label: Enhanced capacities
outcomeTypeOptions.description: Increased the skills, tools, or knowledge of partners.

outcomeTypeOptions.label: Habitat change
outcomeTypeOptions.description: Created measurable change in area, quality, or condition of ecosystems.

outcomeTypeOptions.label: Improved water access
outcomeTypeOptions.description: Changed the population served or the reliability of water access.

outcomeTypeOptions.label: Groundwater resource
outcomeTypeOptions.description: Changed aquifer management, monitoring, or knowledge.

outcomeTypeOptions.label: Other
outcomeTypeOptions.description: Use when the outcome does not fit one of the predefined categories.

toneRules: Accessible to funders and policy audiences.
Avoid internal jargon and unexplained acronyms.
Embed statistics naturally in narrative rather than listing them.

requiredStructure.instruction: Name what SEI did and what it led to.
Explain the most important change, who benefited, and the scale.
Explain why the outcome became possible.
Include the partner, beneficiary, or policymaker quote when available.
Embed both statistics in context when available.
Close with the forward-looking statement on replication, scale, or next-phase potential.

draftTemplateLines: {{project_name_location}} shows how SEI translated technical work into meaningful change. Through {{sei_activities}}, the project contributed to {{primary_outcome_description}}.
The most important result was {{primary_outcome_description}}, benefiting {{beneficiaries_scale}}.

derivedTemplateTokens.impact_stat_2_optional_sentence: If impact_stat_2 exists, render it as a natural follow-on sentence. Otherwise use [SECOND STAT RECOMMENDED].
derivedTemplateTokens.partner_quote_or_placeholder: If partner_quote exists, include it in narrative or as a short pull quote. Otherwise use [QUOTE NEEDED].

deferredElements.note: The source document expects a related link in the final story, but no interview field was defined for it.
